import json
import pandas as pd

# Read the full business data from the JSON file
file_path = var_functions.query_db:6
with open(file_path, 'r') as f:
    business_data = json.load(f)

# Convert to DataFrame for easier processing
df_business = pd.DataFrame(business_data)

# Let's check the structure and sample of description field to understand state extraction
print('Number of businesses:', len(df_business))
print('\nSample descriptions:')
for i, desc in enumerate(df_business['description'].head(10)):
    print(f"{i+1}. {desc}")

# Try to extract state from description
import re

def extract_state(description):
    if not description or description == 'None':
        return None
    # Look for state patterns like "CA,", "CA ", "CA." etc.
    state_match = re.search(r',\s*([A-Z]{2})\s*[,.]|[A-Z]{2}\s+\d{5}', str(description))
    if state_match:
        # Extract the 2-letter state code
        state = state_match.group(1)
        if state and len(state) == 2:
            return state
    return None

# Test state extraction on a few samples
print('\nState extraction test:')
for i, desc in enumerate(df_business['description'].head(10)):
    state = extract_state(desc)
    print(f"{i+1}. State: {state}, Description: {desc}")

# Create a result to return
result = {
    'business_count': len(df_business),
    'sample_descriptions': df_business['description'].head(5).tolist(),
    'sample_states': [extract_state(desc) for desc in df_business['description'].head(10)]
}

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(result))

I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

This location is not one of my favorites people here get pretty rude sometimes no one looks happy it's hit or miss with the food sometimes it's awesome other times terrible I always get honey walnut shrimp for an extra 1.25 which is retarded and Beijing beef I enjoy the rice n the noodles but idk do if u dare
Terrible service. I was charged twice for online order and they refused to refund me. Numerous times this has happened

A cornucupia of flavors and toppings. The area inside is kept clean and tidy. I had a vanilla with oreo. That was heavenly. The space can get crowded in there, so just be aware of that. It's all self-serve, and they let you have samples. Good business.

You can go to Artesano and enjoy the massive metal sculptures made exclusively for this gallery by a single artist in South America, and you can enjoy the large hanging art as well.  Rehabbers will appreciate the meticulous dedo of this old corner on Cresson.  But, for me whose foray into the purchase of original art extends to 3 encounters with minor minor artists, the attraction to Artesano is as a venue for small weddings and all sorts of parties for up to 120 guests.  The two level affair is beautiful, melding old original elements, weathered redone beams and brick with modern touches for wonderful result.  The caterer wont be that happy as they will work out of cramped quarters on the lower level, but its not that bad.   I should say all types of parties but Bar/Bat Mitzvahs as the hands off policy is super strict even though the metal sculptures will do more harm to a thirteen year old than they can do to it, but the wall hangings and fit and finish are not that durable.

Went today for my birthday!First blowout and my hair looks hot! Makeup done as well and it also is fabulous! I didn't have to wait at all got right is and was done at the appropriate time! I will be back ...even for just a scalp massage.

Tuesday for $2 pint night is the night to come to the fox. Everything on tap counts if they have it, but they do tend to run out of some of the good stuff. Food is ok... Got the steak tacos the other night, don't expect many dressings like sour cream, guac, or a lot of flavor. They also don't give you much  salsa, but it's edible.

Pretty sure this was the best meal I've ever had in my life. And that's saying something b/c I've traveled a lot, and I take my food seriously! I'm not a big seafood fan, to be honest nothing on the menu sounded super great. So I went for it and dove straight in to the 7 course chef's tasting menu. Best decision I've ever made. Highly recommend this place for a splurge.

val_args.code:
import json
import pandas as pd

# Read the full business data from the JSON file
file_path = var_functions.query_db:6
with open(file_path, 'r') as f:
    business_data = json.load(f)

# Convert to DataFrame for easier processing
df_business = pd.DataFrame(business_data)

# Let's check the structure and sample of description field to understand state extraction
print('Number of businesses:', len(df_business))
print('\nSample descriptions:')
for i, desc in enumerate(df_business['description'].head(10)):
    print(f"{i+1}. {desc}")

# Try to extract state from description
import re

def extract_state(description):
    if not description or description == 'None':
        return None
    # Look for state patterns like "CA,", "CA ", "CA." etc.
    state_match = re.search(r',\s*([A-Z]{2})\s*[,.]|[A-Z]{2}\s+\d{5}', str(description))
    if state_match:
        # Extract the 2-letter state code
        state = state_match.group(1)
        if state and len(state) == 2:
            return state
    return None

# Test state extraction on a few samples
print('\nState extraction test:')
for i, desc in enumerate(df_business['description'].head(10)):
    state = extract_state(desc)
    print(f"{i+1}. State: {state}, Description: {desc}")

# Create a result to return
result = {
    'business_count': len(df_business),
    'sample_descriptions': df_business['description'].head(5).tolist(),
    'sample_states': [extract_state(desc) for desc in df_business['description'].head(10)]
}

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(result))

var_functions.query_db:5.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

This location is not one of my favorites people here get pretty rude sometimes no one looks happy it's hit or miss with the food sometimes it's awesome other times terrible I always get honey walnut shrimp for an extra 1.25 which is retarded and Beijing beef I enjoy the rice n the noodles but idk do if u dare
Terrible service. I was charged twice for online order and they refused to refund me. Numerous times this has happened

A cornucupia of flavors and toppings. The area inside is kept clean and tidy. I had a vanilla with oreo. That was heavenly. The space can get crowded in there, so just be aware of that. It's all self-serve, and they let you have samples. Good business.

You can go to Artesano and enjoy the massive metal sculptures made exclusively for this gallery by a single artist in South America, and you can enjoy the large hanging art as well.  Rehabbers will appreciate the meticulous dedo of this old corner on Cresson.  But, for me whose foray into the purchase of original art extends to 3 encounters with minor minor artists, the attraction to Artesano is as a venue for small weddings and all sorts of parties for up to 120 guests.  The two level affair is beautiful, melding old original elements, weathered redone beams and brick with modern touches for wonderful result.  The caterer wont be that happy as they will work out of cramped quarters on the lower level, but its not that bad.   I should say all types of parties but Bar/Bat Mitzvahs as the hands off policy is super strict even though the metal sculptures will do more harm to a thirteen year old than they can do to it, but the wall hangings and fit and finish are not that durable.

Went today for my birthday!First blowout and my hair looks hot! Makeup done as well and it also is fabulous! I didn't have to wait at all got right is and was done at the appropriate time! I will be back ...even for just a scalp massage.

Tuesday for $2 pint night is the night to come to the fox. Everything on tap counts if they have it, but they do tend to run out of some of the good stuff. Food is ok... Got the steak tacos the other night, don't expect many dressings like sour cream, guac, or a lot of flavor. They also don't give you much  salsa, but it's edible.

Pretty sure this was the best meal I've ever had in my life. And that's saying something b/c I've traveled a lot, and I take my food seriously! I'm not a big seafood fan, to be honest nothing on the menu sounded super great. So I went for it and dove straight in to the 7 course chef's tasting menu. Best decision I've ever made. Highly recommend this place for a splurge.

args.code:
import json
import pandas as pd

# Read the full business data from the JSON file
file_path = var_functions.query_db:6
with open(file_path, 'r') as f:
    business_data = json.load(f)

# Convert to DataFrame for easier processing
df_business = pd.DataFrame(business_data)

# Import regex module
import re

def extract_state(description):
    if not description or description == 'None':
        return None
    # Look for state patterns like "CA,", "CA ", "CA." etc.
    state_match = re.search(r',\s*([A-Z]{2})\s*[,.]|[A-Z]{2}\s+\d{5}', str(description))
    if state_match:
        # Extract the 2-letter state code
        state = state_match.group(1)
        if state and len(state) == 2:
            return state
    return None

# Apply state extraction to all businesses
df_business['state'] = df_business['description'].apply(extract_state)

# Count reviews per state (using review_count field from business collection)
# First convert review_count to numeric
df_business['review_count'] = pd.to_numeric(df_business['review_count'], errors='coerce')

# Group by state and sum review counts
state_review_counts = df_business.groupby('state')['review_count'].sum().sort_values(ascending=False)

print('Top 10 states by total review count:')
print(state_review_counts.head(10))

print('\nNumber of businesses with state info:', df_business['state'].notna().sum())
print('Number of businesses without state info:', df_business['state'].isna().sum())

# Create a result to return
result = {
    'top_states': state_review_counts.head(10).to_dict(),
    'businesses_with_state': int(df_business['state'].notna().sum()),
    'total_businesses': len(df_business)
}

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(result))

args.code:
import json
import pandas as pd
import re

# Access the business data from the previous query
business_data = var_functions.query_db:6

# Convert to DataFrame for easier processing
df_business = pd.DataFrame(business_data)

# Print info about the data
print(f'Total businesses: {len(df_business)}')
print(f'Columns: {list(df_business.columns)}')

# Function to extract state from description
def extract_state(description):
    if not description or description == 'None':
        return None
    desc_str = str(description)
    # Look for patterns like ", CA" or ", CA." or "CA " with zipcode
    state_match = re.search(r',\s*([A-Z]{2})\s*[,.]|\b([A-Z]{2})\s+\d{5}', desc_str)
    if state_match:
        # Return the captured state code
        return state_match.group(1) or state_match.group(2)
    return None

# Apply state extraction
df_business['state'] = df_business['description'].apply(extract_state)

# Sample check of state extraction
print('\nSample state extraction:')
for i in range(min(10, len(df_business))):
    desc = df_business.iloc[i]['description']
    state = df_business.iloc[i]['state']
    print(f'{i+1}. State: {state} | {desc[:80]}...')

# Convert review_count to numeric
df_business['review_count'] = pd.to_numeric(df_business['review_count'], errors='coerce')

# Group by state and sum review counts
state_review_counts = df_business.groupby('state')['review_count'].sum().sort_values(ascending=False)

print('\nTop 10 states by total review count:')
print(state_review_counts.head(10))

# Prepare result
result = {
    'total_businesses': len(df_business),
    'businesses_with_state': int(df_business['state'].notna().sum()),
    'top_states': state_review_counts.head(10).to_dict()
}

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(result))

var_functions.query_db:5.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

args.code:
import json
import pandas as pd
import re

# Access the business data from the previous query
business_data = var_functions.query_db:6

# Convert to DataFrame for easier processing
df_business = pd.DataFrame(business_data)

# Function to extract state from description
def extract_state(description):
    if not description or description == 'None':
        return None
    desc_str = str(description)
    # Look for state pattern: comma followed by space and 2 uppercase letters
    state_match = re.search(r',\s*([A-Z]{2})\s*[,\.]?', desc_str)
    if state_match:
        return state_match.group(1)
    return None

# Apply state extraction
df_business['state'] = df_business['description'].apply(extract_state)

# Convert review_count to numeric
df_business['review_count'] = pd.to_numeric(df_business['review_count'], errors='coerce')

# Group by state and sum review counts
state_review_counts = df_business.groupby('state')['review_count'].sum().sort_values(ascending=False)

# Sample check
print('Sample states extracted:')
for i in range(min(5, len(df_business))):
    state = df_business.iloc[i]['state']
    review_count = df_business.iloc[i]['review_count']
    print(f'State: {state}, Reviews: {review_count}')

print('\nTop states by total reviews:')
print(state_review_counts.head(10))

# Find state with highest total reviews
if not state_review_counts.empty:
    top_state = state_review_counts.index[0]
    top_state_reviews = state_review_counts.iloc[0]
    print(f'\nTop state: {top_state} with {top_state_reviews} total reviews')
else:
    print('\nNo state data found')

# Prepare result
result = {
    'total_businesses': len(df_business),
    'businesses_with_state': int(df_business['state'].notna().sum()),
    'top_state': state_review_counts.index[0] if not state_review_counts.empty else None,
    'top_state_total_reviews': int(state_review_counts.iloc[0]) if not state_review_counts.empty else 0
}

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(result))

I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

This location is not one of my favorites people here get pretty rude sometimes no one looks happy it's hit or miss with the food sometimes it's awesome other times terrible I always get honey walnut shrimp for an extra 1.25 which is retarded and Beijing beef I enjoy the rice n the noodles but idk do if u dare
Terrible service. I was charged twice for online order and they refused to refund me. Numerous times this has happened

A cornucupia of flavors and toppings. The area inside is kept clean and tidy. I had a vanilla with oreo. That was heavenly. The space can get crowded in there, so just be aware of that. It's all self-serve, and they let you have samples. Good business.

You can go to Artesano and enjoy the massive metal sculptures made exclusively for this gallery by a single artist in South America, and you can enjoy the large hanging art as well.  Rehabbers will appreciate the meticulous dedo of this old corner on Cresson.  But, for me whose foray into the purchase of original art extends to 3 encounters with minor minor artists, the attraction to Artesano is as a venue for small weddings and all sorts of parties for up to 120 guests.  The two level affair is beautiful, melding old original elements, weathered redone beams and brick with modern touches for wonderful result.  The caterer wont be that happy as they will work out of cramped quarters on the lower level, but its not that bad.   I should say all types of parties but Bar/Bat Mitzvahs as the hands off policy is super strict even though the metal sculptures will do more harm to a thirteen year old than they can do to it, but the wall hangings and fit and finish are not that durable.

Went today for my birthday!First blowout and my hair looks hot! Makeup done as well and it also is fabulous! I didn't have to wait at all got right is and was done at the appropriate time! I will be back ...even for just a scalp massage.

Tuesday for $2 pint night is the night to come to the fox. Everything on tap counts if they have it, but they do tend to run out of some of the good stuff. Food is ok... Got the steak tacos the other night, don't expect many dressings like sour cream, guac, or a lot of flavor. They also don't give you much  salsa, but it's edible.

Pretty sure this was the best meal I've ever had in my life. And that's saying something b/c I've traveled a lot, and I take my food seriously! I'm not a big seafood fan, to be honest nothing on the menu sounded super great. So I went for it and dove straight in to the 7 course chef's tasting menu. Best decision I've ever made. Highly recommend this place for a splurge.

val_args.code:
import json
import pandas as pd
import re

# Access the business data from the previous query
business_data = var_functions.query_db:6

# Convert to DataFrame for easier processing
df_business = pd.DataFrame(business_data)

# Function to extract state from description
def extract_state(description):
    if not description or description == 'None':
        return None
    desc_str = str(description)
    # Look for state pattern: comma followed by space and 2 uppercase letters
    state_match = re.search(r',\s*([A-Z]{2})\s*[,\.]?', desc_str)
    if state_match:
        return state_match.group(1)
    return None

# Apply state extraction
df_business['state'] = df_business['description'].apply(extract_state)

# Convert review_count to numeric
df_business['review_count'] = pd.to_numeric(df_business['review_count'], errors='coerce')

# Group by state and sum review counts
state_review_counts = df_business.groupby('state')['review_count'].sum().sort_values(ascending=False)

# Sample check
print('Sample states extracted:')
for i in range(min(5, len(df_business))):
    state = df_business.iloc[i]['state']
    review_count = df_business.iloc[i]['review_count']
    print(f'State: {state}, Reviews: {review_count}')

print('\nTop states by total reviews:')
print(state_review_counts.head(10))

# Find state with highest total reviews
if not state_review_counts.empty:
    top_state = state_review_counts.index[0]
    top_state_reviews = state_review_counts.iloc[0]
    print(f'\nTop state: {top_state} with {top_state_reviews} total reviews')
else:
    print('\nNo state data found')

# Prepare result
result = {
    'total_businesses': len(df_business),
    'businesses_with_state': int(df_business['state'].notna().sum()),
    'top_state': state_review_counts.index[0] if not state_review_counts.empty else None,
    'top_state_total_reviews': int(state_review_counts.iloc[0]) if not state_review_counts.empty else 0
}

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(result))

var_functions.query_db:5.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

This location is not one of my favorites people here get pretty rude sometimes no one looks happy it's hit or miss with the food sometimes it's awesome other times terrible I always get honey walnut shrimp for an extra 1.25 which is retarded and Beijing beef I enjoy the rice n the noodles but idk do if u dare
Terrible service. I was charged twice for online order and they refused to refund me. Numerous times this has happened

A cornucupia of flavors and toppings. The area inside is kept clean and tidy. I had a vanilla with oreo. That was heavenly. The space can get crowded in there, so just be aware of that. It's all self-serve, and they let you have samples. Good business.

You can go to Artesano and enjoy the massive metal sculptures made exclusively for this gallery by a single artist in South America, and you can enjoy the large hanging art as well.  Rehabbers will appreciate the meticulous dedo of this old corner on Cresson.  But, for me whose foray into the purchase of original art extends to 3 encounters with minor minor artists, the attraction to Artesano is as a venue for small weddings and all sorts of parties for up to 120 guests.  The two level affair is beautiful, melding old original elements, weathered redone beams and brick with modern touches for wonderful result.  The caterer wont be that happy as they will work out of cramped quarters on the lower level, but its not that bad.   I should say all types of parties but Bar/Bat Mitzvahs as the hands off policy is super strict even though the metal sculptures will do more harm to a thirteen year old than they can do to it, but the wall hangings and fit and finish are not that durable.

Went today for my birthday!First blowout and my hair looks hot! Makeup done as well and it also is fabulous! I didn't have to wait at all got right is and was done at the appropriate time! I will be back ...even for just a scalp massage.

Tuesday for $2 pint night is the night to come to the fox. Everything on tap counts if they have it, but they do tend to run out of some of the good stuff. Food is ok... Got the steak tacos the other night, don't expect many dressings like sour cream, guac, or a lot of flavor. They also don't give you much  salsa, but it's edible.

Pretty sure this was the best meal I've ever had in my life. And that's saying something b/c I've traveled a lot, and I take my food seriously! I'm not a big seafood fan, to be honest nothing on the menu sounded super great. So I went for it and dove straight in to the 7 course chef's tasting menu. Best decision I've ever made. Highly recommend this place for a splurge.

args.code:
import json
import pandas as pd
import re

# Read the business data from the JSON file
business_file = var_functions.query_db:20
with open(business_file, 'r') as f:
    business_data = json.load(f)

# Convert to DataFrame
df_business = pd.DataFrame(business_data)

# Extract state from description
def extract_state(description):
    if not description or description == 'None':
        return None
    desc_str = str(description)
    # Look for state pattern: comma followed by space and 2 uppercase letters
    state_match = re.search(r',\s*([A-Z]{2})\s*[,\.]?', desc_str)
    if state_match:
        return state_match.group(1)
    return None

# Apply state extraction
df_business['state'] = df_business['description'].apply(extract_state)

# Convert review_count to numeric
df_business['review_count'] = pd.to_numeric(df_business['review_count'], errors='coerce')

# Group by state and sum review counts
state_review_counts = df_business.groupby('state')['review_count'].sum().sort_values(ascending=False)

# Remove null/None state
state_review_counts = state_review_counts.dropna()

# Results
print('Total businesses:', len(df_business))
print('Businesses with state info:', df_business['state'].notna().sum())
print('\nTop 10 states by total review count:')
print(state_review_counts.head(10))

# Get all reviews data to calculate average ratings
print('\nNow fetching review data...')

# Prepare result for next steps
result = {
    'business_file': business_file,
    'total_businesses': int(len(df_business)),
    'businesses_with_state': int(df_business['state'].notna().sum()),
    'top_state': str(state_review_counts.index[0]) if len(state_review_counts) > 0 else None,
    'top_state_total_reviews': int(state_review_counts.iloc[0]) if len(state_review_counts) > 0 else 0,
    'states_ranking': state_review_counts.head(10).to_dict()
}

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(result))

args.code:
import json
import pandas as pd
import re

# Read the business data
business_file = var_functions.query_db:20
with open(business_file, 'r') as f:
    business_data = json.load(f)

df_business = pd.DataFrame(business_data)

def extract_state(description):
    if not description or description == 'None':
        return None
    state_match = re.search(r',\s*([A-Z]{2})\s*[,\.]?', str(description))
    if state_match:
        return state_match.group(1)
    return None

df_business['state'] = df_business['description'].apply(extract_state)
df_business['review_count'] = pd.to_numeric(df_business['review_count'], errors='coerce')
state_review_counts = df_business.groupby('state')['review_count'].sum().sort_values(ascending=False)
state_review_counts = state_review_counts.dropna()

total_businesses = len(df_business)
businesses_with_state = df_business['state'].notna().sum()
top_state = str(state_review_counts.index[0]) if len(state_review_counts) > 0 else None
top_state_reviews = int(state_review_counts.iloc[0]) if len(state_review_counts) > 0 else 0

result = {
    'business_file': business_file,
    'total_businesses': total_businesses,
    'businesses_with_state': int(businesses_with_state),
    'top_state': top_state,
    'top_state_total_reviews': top_state_reviews
}

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(result))

var_functions.query_db:5.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

This location is not one of my favorites people here get pretty rude sometimes no one looks happy it's hit or miss with the food sometimes it's awesome other times terrible I always get honey walnut shrimp for an extra 1.25 which is retarded and Beijing beef I enjoy the rice n the noodles but idk do if u dare
Terrible service. I was charged twice for online order and they refused to refund me. Numerous times this has happened

A cornucupia of flavors and toppings. The area inside is kept clean and tidy. I had a vanilla with oreo. That was heavenly. The space can get crowded in there, so just be aware of that. It's all self-serve, and they let you have samples. Good business.

You can go to Artesano and enjoy the massive metal sculptures made exclusively for this gallery by a single artist in South America, and you can enjoy the large hanging art as well.  Rehabbers will appreciate the meticulous dedo of this old corner on Cresson.  But, for me whose foray into the purchase of original art extends to 3 encounters with minor minor artists, the attraction to Artesano is as a venue for small weddings and all sorts of parties for up to 120 guests.  The two level affair is beautiful, melding old original elements, weathered redone beams and brick with modern touches for wonderful result.  The caterer wont be that happy as they will work out of cramped quarters on the lower level, but its not that bad.   I should say all types of parties but Bar/Bat Mitzvahs as the hands off policy is super strict even though the metal sculptures will do more harm to a thirteen year old than they can do to it, but the wall hangings and fit and finish are not that durable.

Went today for my birthday!First blowout and my hair looks hot! Makeup done as well and it also is fabulous! I didn't have to wait at all got right is and was done at the appropriate time! I will be back ...even for just a scalp massage.

Tuesday for $2 pint night is the night to come to the fox. Everything on tap counts if they have it, but they do tend to run out of some of the good stuff. Food is ok... Got the steak tacos the other night, don't expect many dressings like sour cream, guac, or a lot of flavor. They also don't give you much  salsa, but it's edible.

Pretty sure this was the best meal I've ever had in my life. And that's saying something b/c I've traveled a lot, and I take my food seriously! I'm not a big seafood fan, to be honest nothing on the menu sounded super great. So I went for it and dove straight in to the 7 course chef's tasting menu. Best decision I've ever made. Highly recommend this place for a splurge.

args.code:
import json
import pandas as pd
import re

# Read the business data from the file
business_file = var_functions.query_db:34
with open(business_file, 'r') as f:
    business_data = json.load(f)

print(f'Loaded {len(business_data)} businesses')

# Extract state from description
def extract_state(description):
    if not description or description == 'None':
        return None
    desc_str = str(description)
    # Look for pattern like ", CA," or ", CA."
    state_match = re.search(r',\s*([A-Z]{2})\s*[,\.]?', desc_str)
    if state_match:
        return state_match.group(1)
    return None

# Apply state extraction
for business in business_data:
    business['state'] = extract_state(business.get('description', ''))
    business['review_count'] = int(business.get('review_count', 0))

# Count reviews per state
state_reviews = {}
for business in business_data:
    state = business['state']
    if state:
        state_reviews[state] = state_reviews.get(state, 0) + business['review_count']

# Sort by total reviews
sorted_states = sorted(state_reviews.items(), key=lambda x: x[1], reverse=True)

print(f'Found {len(sorted_states)} states with businesses')
print('Top 10 states by total reviews:')
for state, reviews in sorted_states[:10]:
    print(f'  {state}: {reviews} reviews')

# Get top state
top_state = sorted_states[0][0] if sorted_states else None
top_state_total_reviews = sorted_states[0][1] if sorted_states else 0

result = {
    'business_count': len(business_data),
    'states_count': len(sorted_states),
    'top_state': top_state,
    'top_state_total_reviews': top_state_total_reviews,
    'all_states': sorted_states[:10]
}

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(result))

var_functions.query_db:5.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

This location is not one of my favorites people here get pretty rude sometimes no one looks happy it's hit or miss with the food sometimes it's awesome other times terrible I always get honey walnut shrimp for an extra 1.25 which is retarded and Beijing beef I enjoy the rice n the noodles but idk do if u dare
Terrible service. I was charged twice for online order and they refused to refund me. Numerous times this has happened

A cornucupia of flavors and toppings. The area inside is kept clean and tidy. I had a vanilla with oreo. That was heavenly. The space can get crowded in there, so just be aware of that. It's all self-serve, and they let you have samples. Good business.

You can go to Artesano and enjoy the massive metal sculptures made exclusively for this gallery by a single artist in South America, and you can enjoy the large hanging art as well.  Rehabbers will appreciate the meticulous dedo of this old corner on Cresson.  But, for me whose foray into the purchase of original art extends to 3 encounters with minor minor artists, the attraction to Artesano is as a venue for small weddings and all sorts of parties for up to 120 guests.  The two level affair is beautiful, melding old original elements, weathered redone beams and brick with modern touches for wonderful result.  The caterer wont be that happy as they will work out of cramped quarters on the lower level, but its not that bad.   I should say all types of parties but Bar/Bat Mitzvahs as the hands off policy is super strict even though the metal sculptures will do more harm to a thirteen year old than they can do to it, but the wall hangings and fit and finish are not that durable.

Went today for my birthday!First blowout and my hair looks hot! Makeup done as well and it also is fabulous! I didn't have to wait at all got right is and was done at the appropriate time! I will be back ...even for just a scalp massage.

Tuesday for $2 pint night is the night to come to the fox. Everything on tap counts if they have it, but they do tend to run out of some of the good stuff. Food is ok... Got the steak tacos the other night, don't expect many dressings like sour cream, guac, or a lot of flavor. They also don't give you much  salsa, but it's edible.

Pretty sure this was the best meal I've ever had in my life. And that's saying something b/c I've traveled a lot, and I take my food seriously! I'm not a big seafood fan, to be honest nothing on the menu sounded super great. So I went for it and dove straight in to the 7 course chef's tasting menu. Best decision I've ever made. Highly recommend this place for a splurge.

print('Top 10 states by total reviews:')
for state, reviews in sorted_states[:10]:
    print(f'  {state}: {reviews} reviews')

# Get top state
top_state = sorted_states[0][0] if sorted_states else None
top_state_total_reviews = sorted_states[0][1] if sorted_states else 0

result = {
    'business_count': len(business_data),
    'states_count': len(sorted_states),
    'top_state': top_state,
    'top_state_total_reviews': top_state_total_reviews,
    'all_states': sorted_states[:10]
}

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(result))

var_functions.query_db:5.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

This location is not one of my favorites people here get pretty rude sometimes no one looks happy it's hit or miss with the food sometimes it's awesome other times terrible I always get honey walnut shrimp for an extra 1.25 which is retarded and Beijing beef I enjoy the rice n the noodles but idk do if u dare
Terrible service. I was charged twice for online order and they refused to refund me. Numerous times this has happened

A cornucupia of flavors and toppings. The area inside is kept clean and tidy. I had a vanilla with oreo. That was heavenly. The space can get crowded in there, so just be aware of that. It's all self-serve, and they let you have samples. Good business.

You can go to Artesano and enjoy the massive metal sculptures made exclusively for this gallery by a single artist in South America, and you can enjoy the large hanging art as well.  Rehabbers will appreciate the meticulous dedo of this old corner on Cresson.  But, for me whose foray into the purchase of original art extends to 3 encounters with minor minor artists, the attraction to Artesano is as a venue for small weddings and all sorts of parties for up to 120 guests.  The two level affair is beautiful, melding old original elements, weathered redone beams and brick with modern touches for wonderful result.  The caterer wont be that happy as they will work out of cramped quarters on the lower level, but its not that bad.   I should say all types of parties but Bar/Bat Mitzvahs as the hands off policy is super strict even though the metal sculptures will do more harm to a thirteen year old than they can do to it, but the wall hangings and fit and finish are not that durable.

Went today for my birthday!First blowout and my hair looks hot! Makeup done as well and it also is fabulous! I didn't have to wait at all got right is and was done at the appropriate time! I will be back ...even for just a scalp massage.

Tuesday for $2 pint night is the night to come to the fox. Everything on tap counts if they have it, but they do tend to run out of some of the good stuff. Food is ok... Got the steak tacos the other night, don't expect many dressings like sour cream, guac, or a lot of flavor. They also don't give you much  salsa, but it's edible.

Pretty sure this was the best meal I've ever had in my life. And that's saying something b/c I've traveled a lot, and I take my food seriously! I'm not a big seafood fan, to be honest nothing on the menu sounded super great. So I went for it and dove straight in to the 7 course chef's tasting menu. Best decision I've ever made. Highly recommend this place for a splurge.

args.code:
import json
import re

# Read the business data
business_file = var_functions.query_db:38
with open(business_file, 'r') as f:
    business_data = json.load(f)

print(f'Total businesses loaded: {len(business_data)}')

# Extract state from description and sum review counts by state
state_reviews = {}
for business in business_data:
    description = business.get('description', '')
    review_count = int(business.get('review_count', 0))
    
    # Extract 2-letter state code from description
    state_match = re.search(r',\s*([A-Z]{2})\s*[,\.]?', description)
    if state_match:
        state = state_match.group(1)
        state_reviews[state] = state_reviews.get(state, 0) + review_count

# Sort states by total review count
sorted_states = sorted(state_reviews.items(), key=lambda x: x[1], reverse=True)

print(f'Found {len(sorted_states)} states')
print('\nTop 5 states by total reviews:')
for state, total_reviews in sorted_states[:5]:
    print(f'  {state}: {total_reviews} reviews')

# Get the top state
top_state = sorted_states[0][0] if sorted_states else None
top_state_total_reviews = sorted_states[0][1] if sorted_states else 0

result = {
    'top_state': top_state,
    'top_state_total_reviews': top_state_total_reviews,
    'all_states_count': len(sorted_states),
    'top_5_states': sorted_states[:5]
}

print('\n__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(result))

val_args.code:
import json
import re

# Read the business data
business_file = var_functions.query_db:38
with open(business_file, 'r') as f:
    business_data = json.load(f)

print(f'Total businesses loaded: {len(business_data)}')

# Extract state from description and sum review counts by state
state_reviews = {}
for business in business_data:
    description = business.get('description', '')
    review_count = int(business.get('review_count', 0))
    
    # Extract 2-letter state code from description
    state_match = re.search(r',\s*([A-Z]{2})\s*[,\.]?', description)
    if state_match:
        state = state_match.group(1)
        state_reviews[state] = state_reviews.get(state, 0) + review_count

# Sort states by total review count
sorted_states = sorted(state_reviews.items(), key=lambda x: x[1], reverse=True)

print(f'Found {len(sorted_states)} states')
print('\nTop 5 states by total reviews:')
for state, total_reviews in sorted_states[:5]:
    print(f'  {state}: {total_reviews} reviews')

# Get the top state
top_state = sorted_states[0][0] if sorted_states else None
top_state_total_reviews = sorted_states[0][1] if sorted_states else 0

result = {
    'top_state': top_state,
    'top_state_total_reviews': top_state_total_reviews,
    'all_states_count': len(sorted_states),
    'top_5_states': sorted_states[:5]
}

print('\n__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(result))

var_functions.query_db:5.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

This location is not one of my favorites people here get pretty rude sometimes no one looks happy it's hit or miss with the food sometimes it's awesome other times terrible I always get honey walnut shrimp for an extra 1.25 which is retarded and Beijing beef I enjoy the rice n the noodles but idk do if u dare
Terrible service. I was charged twice for online order and they refused to refund me. Numerous times this has happened

A cornucupia of flavors and toppings. The area inside is kept clean and tidy. I had a vanilla with oreo. That was heavenly. The space can get crowded in there, so just be aware of that. It's all self-serve, and they let you have samples. Good business.

You can go to Artesano and enjoy the massive metal sculptures made exclusively for this gallery by a single artist in South America, and you can enjoy the large hanging art as well.  Rehabbers will appreciate the meticulous dedo of this old corner on Cresson.  But, for me whose foray into the purchase of original art extends to 3 encounters with minor minor artists, the attraction to Artesano is as a venue for small weddings and all sorts of parties for up to 120 guests.  The two level affair is beautiful, melding old original elements, weathered redone beams and brick with modern touches for wonderful result.  The caterer wont be that happy as they will work out of cramped quarters on the lower level, but its not that bad.   I should say all types of parties but Bar/Bat Mitzvahs as the hands off policy is super strict even though the metal sculptures will do more harm to a thirteen year old than they can do to it, but the wall hangings and fit and finish are not that durable.

Went today for my birthday!First blowout and my hair looks hot! Makeup done as well and it also is fabulous! I didn't have to wait at all got right is and was done at the appropriate time! I will be back ...even for just a scalp massage.

Tuesday for $2 pint night is the night to come to the fox. Everything on tap counts if they have it, but they do tend to run out of some of the good stuff. Food is ok... Got the steak tacos the other night, don't expect many dressings like sour cream, guac, or a lot of flavor. They also don't give you much  salsa, but it's edible.

Pretty sure this was the best meal I've ever had in my life. And that's saying something b/c I've traveled a lot, and I take my food seriously! I'm not a big seafood fan, to be honest nothing on the menu sounded super great. So I went for it and dove straight in to the 7 course chef's tasting menu. Best decision I've ever made. Highly recommend this place for a splurge.

args.code:
import json
import re

business_file = var_functions.query_db:44

with open(business_file, 'r') as f:
    business_data = json.load(f)

print('Total businesses:', len(business_data))

# Extract state and sum review counts
state_reviews = {}
for business in business_data:
    description = business.get('description', '')
    review_count = int(business.get('review_count', 0))
    
    # Extract STATE from description like "Located at ... in City, STATE, ..."
    match = re.search(r',\s*([A-Z]{2})\s*[,\.]', description)
    if match:
        state = match.group(1)
        state_reviews[state] = state_reviews.get(state, 0) + review_count

# Sort states by total reviews
sorted_states = sorted(state_reviews.items(), key=lambda x: x[1], reverse=True)

print(f'Found {len(sorted_states)} states with businesses')
print('Top 10 states:')
for state, reviews in sorted_states[:10]:
    print(f'  {state}: {reviews} reviews')

# Get top state
top_state = sorted_states[0][0] if sorted_states else None
top_state_reviews = sorted_states[0][1] if sorted_states else 0

result = {
    'top_state': top_state,
    'top_state_total_reviews': top_state_reviews,
    'all_states': sorted_states
}

print('\n__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(result))

var_functions.query_db:5.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

This location is not one of my favorites people here get pretty rude sometimes no one looks happy it's hit or miss with the food sometimes it's awesome other times terrible I always get honey walnut shrimp for an extra 1.25 which is retarded and Beijing beef I enjoy the rice n the noodles but idk do if u dare
Terrible service. I was charged twice for online order and they refused to refund me. Numerous times this has happened

A cornucupia of flavors and toppings. The area inside is kept clean and tidy. I had a vanilla with oreo. That was heavenly. The space can get crowded in there, so just be aware of that. It's all self-serve, and they let you have samples. Good business.

You can go to Artesano and enjoy the massive metal sculptures made exclusively for this gallery by a single artist in South America, and you can enjoy the large hanging art as well.  Rehabbers will appreciate the meticulous dedo of this old corner on Cresson.  But, for me whose foray into the purchase of original art extends to 3 encounters with minor minor artists, the attraction to Artesano is as a venue for small weddings and all sorts of parties for up to 120 guests.  The two level affair is beautiful, melding old original elements, weathered redone beams and brick with modern touches for wonderful result.  The caterer wont be that happy as they will work out of cramped quarters on the lower level, but its not that bad.   I should say all types of parties but Bar/Bat Mitzvahs as the hands off policy is super strict even though the metal sculptures will do more harm to a thirteen year old than they can do to it, but the wall hangings and fit and finish are not that durable.

Went today for my birthday!First blowout and my hair looks hot! Makeup done as well and it also is fabulous! I didn't have to wait at all got right is and was done at the appropriate time! I will be back ...even for just a scalp massage.

Tuesday for $2 pint night is the night to come to the fox. Everything on tap counts if they have it, but they do tend to run out of some of the good stuff. Food is ok... Got the steak tacos the other night, don't expect many dressings like sour cream, guac, or a lot of flavor. They also don't give you much  salsa, but it's edible.

Pretty sure this was the best meal I've ever had in my life. And that's saying something b/c I've traveled a lot, and I take my food seriously! I'm not a big seafood fan, to be honest nothing on the menu sounded super great. So I went for it and dove straight in to the 7 course chef's tasting menu. Best decision I've ever made. Highly recommend this place for a splurge.

val_args.code:
import json
import re

business_file = var_functions.query_db:44

with open(business_file, 'r') as f:
    business_data = json.load(f)

print('Total businesses:', len(business_data))

# Extract state and sum review counts
state_reviews = {}
for business in business_data:
    description = business.get('description', '')
    review_count = int(business.get('review_count', 0))
    
    # Extract STATE from description like "Located at ... in City, STATE, ..."
    match = re.search(r',\s*([A-Z]{2})\s*[,\.]', description)
    if match:
        state = match.group(1)
        state_reviews[state] = state_reviews.get(state, 0) + review_count

# Sort states by total reviews
sorted_states = sorted(state_reviews.items(), key=lambda x: x[1], reverse=True)

print(f'Found {len(sorted_states)} states with businesses')
print('Top 10 states:')
for state, reviews in sorted_states[:10]:
    print(f'  {state}: {reviews} reviews')

# Get top state
top_state = sorted_states[0][0] if sorted_states else None
top_state_reviews = sorted_states[0][1] if sorted_states else 0

result = {
    'top_state': top_state,
    'top_state_total_reviews': top_state_reviews,
    'all_states': sorted_states
}

print('\n__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(result))

args.code:
import json
import re

# Read business data
business_file = var_functions.query_db:44
with open(business_file, 'r') as f:
    business_data = json.load(f)

print('Total businesses:', len(business_data))

# Extract state and sum review counts by state
state_reviews = {}
for business in business_data:
    description = business.get('description', '')
    review_count = int(business.get('review_count', 0))
    
    # Extract state pattern like ", CA," or ", CA."
    match = re.search(r',\s*([A-Z]{2})\s*[,\.]', description)
    if match:
        state = match.group(1)
        state_reviews[state] = state_reviews.get(state, 0) + review_count

# Sort and find top state
sorted_states = sorted(state_reviews.items(), key=lambda x: x[1], reverse=True)
top_state = sorted_states[0][0] if sorted_states else None
top_state_reviews = sorted_states[0][1] if sorted_states else 0

print('States found:', len(sorted_states))
print('Top state:', top_state)
print('Total reviews in top state:', top_state_reviews)

result = {
    'top_state': top_state,
    'top_state_total_reviews': top_state_reviews,
    'businesses_count': len(business_data)
}

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(result))

var_functions.query_db:5.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

This location is not one of my favorites people here get pretty rude sometimes no one looks happy it's hit or miss with the food sometimes it's awesome other times terrible I always get honey walnut shrimp for an extra 1.25 which is retarded and Beijing beef I enjoy the rice n the noodles but idk do if u dare
Terrible service. I was charged twice for online order and they refused to refund me. Numerous times this has happened

A cornucupia of flavors and toppings. The area inside is kept clean and tidy. I had a vanilla with oreo. That was heavenly. The space can get crowded in there, so just be aware of that. It's all self-serve, and they let you have samples. Good business.

You can go to Artesano and enjoy the massive metal sculptures made exclusively for this gallery by a single artist in South America, and you can enjoy the large hanging art as well.  Rehabbers will appreciate the meticulous dedo of this old corner on Cresson.  But, for me whose foray into the purchase of original art extends to 3 encounters with minor minor artists, the attraction to Artesano is as a venue for small weddings and all sorts of parties for up to 120 guests.  The two level affair is beautiful, melding old original elements, weathered redone beams and brick with modern touches for wonderful result.  The caterer wont be that happy as they will work out of cramped quarters on the lower level, but its not that bad.   I should say all types of parties but Bar/Bat Mitzvahs as the hands off policy is super strict even though the metal sculptures will do more harm to a thirteen year old than they can do to it, but the wall hangings and fit and finish are not that durable.

Went today for my birthday!First blowout and my hair looks hot! Makeup done as well and it also is fabulous! I didn't have to wait at all got right is and was done at the appropriate time! I will be back ...even for just a scalp massage.

Tuesday for $2 pint night is the night to come to the fox. Everything on tap counts if they have it, but they do tend to run out of some of the good stuff. Food is ok... Got the steak tacos the other night, don't expect many dressings like sour cream, guac, or a lot of flavor. They also don't give you much  salsa, but it's edible.

Pretty sure this was the best meal I've ever had in my life. And that's saying something b/c I've traveled a lot, and I take my food seriously! I'm not a big seafood fan, to be honest nothing on the menu sounded super great. So I went for it and dove straight in to the 7 course chef's tasting menu. Best decision I've ever made. Highly recommend this place for a splurge.

args.code:
import json, re

with open(var_functions.query_db:44, 'r') as f:
    business_data = json.load(f)

state_reviews = {}
for biz in business_data:
    desc = biz.get('description', '')
    reviews = int(biz.get('review_count', 0))
    m = re.search(r',\s*([A-Z]{2})\s*[,\.]?', desc)
    if m:
        state = m.group(1)
        state_reviews[state] = state_reviews.get(state, 0) + reviews

sorted_states = sorted(state_reviews.items(), key=lambda x: x[1], reverse=True)
result = {
    'top_state': sorted_states[0][0] if sorted_states else None,
    'top_state_total_reviews': sorted_states[0][1] if sorted_states else 0
}

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(result))

var_functions.query_db:5.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

This location is not one of my favorites people here get pretty rude sometimes no one looks happy it's hit or miss with the food sometimes it's awesome other times terrible I always get honey walnut shrimp for an extra 1.25 which is retarded and Beijing beef I enjoy the rice n the noodles but idk do if u dare
Terrible service. I was charged twice for online order and they refused to refund me. Numerous times this has happened

A cornucupia of flavors and toppings. The area inside is kept clean and tidy. I had a vanilla with oreo. That was heavenly. The space can get crowded in there, so just be aware of that. It's all self-serve, and they let you have samples. Good business.

You can go to Artesano and enjoy the massive metal sculptures made exclusively for this gallery by a single artist in South America, and you can enjoy the large hanging art as well.  Rehabbers will appreciate the meticulous dedo of this old corner on Cresson.  But, for me whose foray into the purchase of original art extends to 3 encounters with minor minor artists, the attraction to Artesano is as a venue for small weddings and all sorts of parties for up to 120 guests.  The two level affair is beautiful, melding old original elements, weathered redone beams and brick with modern touches for wonderful result.  The caterer wont be that happy as they will work out of cramped quarters on the lower level, but its not that bad.   I should say all types of parties but Bar/Bat Mitzvahs as the hands off policy is super strict even though the metal sculptures will do more harm to a thirteen year old than they can do to it, but the wall hangings and fit and finish are not that durable.

Went today for my birthday!First blowout and my hair looks hot! Makeup done as well and it also is fabulous! I didn't have to wait at all got right is and was done at the appropriate time! I will be back ...even for just a scalp massage.

Tuesday for $2 pint night is the night to come to the fox. Everything on tap counts if they have it, but they do tend to run out of some of the good stuff. Food is ok... Got the steak tacos the other night, don't expect many dressings like sour cream, guac, or a lot of flavor. They also don't give you much  salsa, but it's edible.

Pretty sure this was the best meal I've ever had in my life. And that's saying something b/c I've traveled a lot, and I take my food seriously! I'm not a big seafood fan, to be honest nothing on the menu sounded super great. So I went for it and dove straight in to the 7 course chef's tasting menu. Best decision I've ever made. Highly recommend this place for a splurge.

Pretty sure this was the best meal I've ever had in my life. And that's saying something b/c I've traveled a lot, and I take my food seriously! I'm not a big seafood fan, to be honest nothing on the menu sounded super great. So I went for it and dove straight in to the 7 course chef's tasting menu. Best decision I've ever made. Highly recommend this place for a splurge.

args.code:
import json
import re

# Get the business data from the stored variable
business_data = locals()['var_functions.query_db:44']

print('Total businesses:', len(business_data))

# Extract state and sum review counts by state
state_reviews = {}
for business in business_data:
    description = business.get('description', '')
    review_count = int(business.get('review_count', 0))
    
    # Extract state pattern like ", CA," or ", CA."
    match = re.search(r',\s*([A-Z]{2})\s*[,\.]', description)
    if match:
        state = match.group(1)
        state_reviews[state] = state_reviews.get(state, 0) + review_count

# Sort and find top state
sorted_states = sorted(state_reviews.items(), key=lambda x: x[1], reverse=True)

print('States found:', len(sorted_states))
print('Top 10 states:')
for state, reviews in sorted_states[:10]:
    print(f'  {state}: {reviews} reviews')

top_state = sorted_states[0][0] if sorted_states else None
top_state_reviews = sorted_states[0][1] if sorted_states else 0

result = {
    'top_state': top_state,
    'top_state_total_reviews': top_state_reviews,
    'all_states': sorted_states
}

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(result))

var_functions.query_db:5.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

This location is not one of my favorites people here get pretty rude sometimes no one looks happy it's hit or miss with the food sometimes it's awesome other times terrible I always get honey walnut shrimp for an extra 1.25 which is retarded and Beijing beef I enjoy the rice n the noodles but idk do if u dare
Terrible service. I was charged twice for online order and they refused to refund me. Numerous times this has happened

A cornucupia of flavors and toppings. The area inside is kept clean and tidy. I had a vanilla with oreo. That was heavenly. The space can get crowded in there, so just be aware of that. It's all self-serve, and they let you have samples. Good business.

You can go to Artesano and enjoy the massive metal sculptures made exclusively for this gallery by a single artist in South America, and you can enjoy the large hanging art as well.  Rehabbers will appreciate the meticulous dedo of this old corner on Cresson.  But, for me whose foray into the purchase of original art extends to 3 encounters with minor minor artists, the attraction to Artesano is as a venue for small weddings and all sorts of parties for up to 120 guests.  The two level affair is beautiful, melding old original elements, weathered redone beams and brick with modern touches for wonderful result.  The caterer wont be that happy as they will work out of cramped quarters on the lower level, but its not that bad.   I should say all types of parties but Bar/Bat Mitzvahs as the hands off policy is super strict even though the metal sculptures will do more harm to a thirteen year old than they can do to it, but the wall hangings and fit and finish are not that durable.

Went today for my birthday!First blowout and my hair looks hot! Makeup done as well and it also is fabulous! I didn't have to wait at all got right is and was done at the appropriate time! I will be back ...even for just a scalp massage.

Tuesday for $2 pint night is the night to come to the fox. Everything on tap counts if they have it, but they do tend to run out of some of the good stuff. Food is ok... Got the steak tacos the other night, don't expect many dressings like sour cream, guac, or a lot of flavor. They also don't give you much  salsa, but it's edible.

Pretty sure this was the best meal I've ever had in my life. And that's saying something b/c I've traveled a lot, and I take my food seriously! I'm not a big seafood fan, to be honest nothing on the menu sounded super great. So I went for it and dove straight in to the 7 course chef's tasting menu. Best decision I've ever made. Highly recommend this place for a splurge.
